channel.title: 8bitconcepts Research
channel.description: Practitioner-grade research on AI engineering, hiring, compensation, and infrastructure. Auto-refreshed weekly.

item.title: Claude Code CLAUDE.md: The Practical Guide to Project Memory
item.description: CLAUDE.md gives Claude Code persistent project context across sessions. Here is what to put in it, what makes it worse, and the one problem it cannot solve.

item.title: The Agentic Commerce Gap: Why Most Businesses Are Invisible to AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are beginning to shop, quote, and purchase on behalf of users. Most online businesses are architecturally invisible to them. Here is what that means and what to do about it.

item.title: Claude Code Context Limit: Why It Breaks Mid-Task and the Fix
item.description: Claude Code's context window fills up on long sessions and large repos, killing your work mid-task. Here is what actually works for continuing without losing your session.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Compensation by Skill
item.description: Research roles pay a $42k premium over generative-AI roles ($274k vs $231k avg), even though generative-AI has 2.5x more openings.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Hiring by Geography
item.description: Live analysis: 67% of AI/ML roles are in the US, 48% in SF Bay Area alone. European AI salaries 3% below US median ($227,140 vs $234,769). Top 15 cities by AI role count, regional salary, remote vs onsite mix. Auto-regenerated weekly.

item.title: Q2 2026 AI Engineering Hiring Snapshot
item.description: Live snapshot: 8,618 AI/ML engineering roles across 513 companies, $213k median, 599 new this week. OpenAI leads with 336 open roles.

item.title: Q2 2026 The Junior AI Hiring Gap
item.description: Only ~7% of AI/ML engineering roles are open to juniors. For every entry-level opening there are ~10 senior-plus roles - the tightest junior-to-senior ratio in tech.

item.title: Q2 2026 MCP Ecosystem Health
item.description: 5,578 agent-ready sites indexed, only 575 (10.3%) pass a live JSON-RPC handshake. Category breakdown and the regulated verticals still waiting to be built.

item.title: Q2 2026 Remote vs Onsite AI Hiring
item.description: Hybrid AI/ML roles pay a ~$35k premium over remote+onsite ($253k vs $218k). 55% of AI engineering roles still require full onsite attendance.

item.title: The Validation Gap
item.description: Most engineering teams can tell you whether their AI pipeline ran. Almost none can tell you whether it worked. The dominant failure mode in production AI isn't model error — it's silent degradation between pipeline stages that no gate catches.

item.title: The Self-Testing Layer
item.description: A researched white paper on why agentic businesses need self-testing, self-improving systems: artifact scoring, feedback loops, evaluator calibration, audit trails, and regression infrastructure.

item.title: Your AI Is Moving Back Onto the Machine - 8bitConcepts
item.description: The future of AI inference is not cloud versus device. The shift is hierarchy: cloud for frontier work, devices for the everyday intelligence layer close to private context.

item.title: The Compounding Gap
item.description: The adoption gap between fast-moving and slow-moving companies isn't linear -- it compounds. By the time slow movers notice, the lead is structural and unrecoverable. Why 2026 is the last year you can still close it.

item.title: The Context Wall
item.description: AI agents fail 97.5% of real organizational work. The failure mechanism has nothing to do with model quality or coding skill -- it's the missing infrastructure of context. Why solo deployments fail and what they need installed instead.

item.title: The Domain Advantage
item.description: The 20 years of operating expertise you've built is exactly what AI can't replicate. The scarce skill in 2026 is problem framing, not model operation -- which means established operators already have the hardest ingredient. They just need the install on top.

item.title: The Expansion Tax
item.description: If you're using AI to cut costs, you're paying a tax on the real opportunity. When execution costs drop by an order of magnitude, the market itself expands -- and the companies cutting headcount are ceding that new territory to competitors who move in.

item.title: The Foundation Trap
item.description: Every AI architecture decision you make today is a bet on which infrastructure layer survives 2027. Most operators are placing this bet without knowing what they're trading off. Why the cost of a wrong foundation compounds.

item.title: The PNW AI Desert
item.description: There are 8,400+ AI/ML jobs open across the major hubs. Almost none are in Vancouver WA, Portland OR, Camas WA, or Tigard OR. The AI hiring market does not extend to PNW SMB territory -- which has a specific, immediate implication for operating businesses here.

item.title: Beyond the Prompt
item.description: The teams shipping reliable production agentic systems are not prompting harder - they moved through a specific engineering maturity ladder.

item.title: Shift Handoff Intelligence
item.description: 100% information retention with AI-generated shift briefings vs. 40-60% with verbal handoffs. The pattern-detection gap is where preventable failures originate.

item.title: The Agentic Accountability Gap
item.description: Enterprise teams spent three years learning how to stop AI from saying the wrong thing. Then they handed those same systems write-access to production.

item.title: The Governance Vacuum
item.description: Most enterprises now have AI deployed in production. Almost none have decided who owns it when it breaks. Enterprise AI governance has been treated as a documentation problem when it is actually an engineering problem.

item.title: The Guardrails Gap
item.description: Engineering teams spent 2023 and 2024 obsessing over what AI would say. In 2026, the threat has shifted - agentic systems are now taking action.

item.title: The Hallucination Budget
item.description: Most engineering teams ship LLM features with less testing rigor than they apply to a login form. Production hallucinations land on customer trust and legal risk.

item.title: The Integration Tax
item.description: Model API costs are 10-20% of what AI actually costs to ship. Where the other 80% goes.

item.title: The Mandate Trap
item.description: Shopify's AI mandate worked. Duolingo's didn't. Companies copying the Shopify memo template are learning the wrong lesson.

item.title: The Measurement Problem
item.description: A company ran an AI system for eight months before discovering four months of silent degradation. Most have no better detection mechanism.

item.title: The Observability Blind Spot
item.description: Engineering teams spent years building world-class observability for their APIs — then they deployed LLMs and went functionally blind. Traditional APM is categorically unfit for LLM production systems.

item.title: The Org Chart Problem
item.description: AI transformation fails because of where it sits in the org chart. Every placement encodes a ceiling.

item.title: The Rate Limit Ceiling
item.description: Engineering teams obsess over model quality, but the thing quietly killing AI products in production isn't hallucinations or prompt drift — it's infrastructure throttling. When 60% of LLM errors in production traces come from exceeded rate limits, the bottleneck isn't your model. It's your architecture.

item.title: The Six Percent
item.description: 88% of organizations use AI. Only 6% see meaningful returns. What McKinsey found in 2,000 companies across 105 countries.
